title: Why does Shinji follow the conformities of society
display_title: Why does Shinji follow the conformities of society
wikitext:
[]: Why does Shinji follow the conformities of society?             Quote Post by Losstarot » Sun Oct 28, 2007 9:50 pm Shinji before and whine in NERV seem to have problems relating to society; before NERV he did poorly in school and was apathetic and almost nihilistic of life. During NERV he continued this trend and discovered a lot of other issues that he realized (specifically his ideas of society); he seemed to hate most other people and wanted to be alone with just a very small gleam of someone giving him hope. However, even though he felt this hate and isolation from society, he still felt the need to follow the "norms" of society. Shinji never outed himself in any manner and always remained there, as in he was just a blob who went along with society's manner. He always tried to do what he thought society wanted him to do, despite what he really wanted. For example, Shinji believed that he must always fight no matter what he wanted, he couldn't come to terms with his feelings of Kaworu because it was against what was normal, and he tried to be the good boy and not disrupt the flow. He never went against the tide and did anything that he deeply wanted.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Oct 28, 2007 10:45 pm He was probably too afraid to do anything else. Anno described him as being too scared to even kill himself, and he is often seen as being wimpy. I'd say that any form of social rebellion by Shinji would be completely against his character.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Oct 28, 2007 10:49 pm Stan: Actually, I was wrong. You see, Kyle, I learned something, just now. It is good to go with the group. A group mentality is healthy, sometimes. [/south park]

[]:             Quote Post by Losstarot » Sun Oct 28, 2007 10:49 pm I'm not saying an actual rebellion as in not giving himself happiness; he denied going against the grain he though he lost so much in the long run.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Oct 28, 2007 10:50 pm ADV Platinum #03 wrote: IBUKI: But I'm surprised that Shinji agreed to be a pilot. RITSUKO: He simply does what he's told to do. Maybe that's his way of getting through life.

[]: Re: Why does Shinji follow the conformities of society?             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Oct 28, 2007 10:51 pm Losstarot wrote: Shinji before and whine in NERV seem to have problems relating to society

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Oct 28, 2007 10:57 pm the most Shinji did to be rebellious that incident with Shamshel Shinji is scared of what others think of him, if he was rebelious, people might think he's a bad kid or something and not like him, and he doesnt want that. he tries to please others to make them like him

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Mon Oct 29, 2007 1:03 am It's hard to be like Shinji. He is actually composed of two parts of himself: one that is a regular, normal/average kid, as we see in the dream sequence, where is as normal as can be, and the entirely depressed side, that derails his thoughts and efforts to contact the outside word, e.g., other people. THIS creates the Hedgehog's dilemma, and although he is not so far down the road as to have split personality syndrome, each of these "Shinjis" have an effect on his thinking and otherwise. Before Leliel's consumption of Shinji, his happy side was pretty dominant. He showed bravery, a sense of pride, and visual cues that he is happy (grinning, smiling, happy talking). The following episodes from that are all downhill, and his sadder side takes over. The influence of these two sides cause him not to ride down a Eva-Elevator with a Harley Davidson motorcycle and a parachute and an expensive pair of sunglasses or leveling Central Dogma to the last man the second he synchonizes with Eva01.

[]: Re: Why does Shinji follow the conformities of society?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Oct 29, 2007 7:11 am Losstarot wrote: he seemed to hate most other people and wanted to be alone with just a very small gleam of someone giving him hope.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Mon Oct 29, 2007 2:36 pm Bottom line: Shinji's a nice mess.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 29, 2007 2:47 pm Shinji is the type who hates himself, NOT other people. On the contrary, he cares deeply about other people, but loses that feeling when he feels betrayed in EoE ("lol Everybody just die."). Also, what Eva Yojimbo (who's right on the money with all this ;)) said. before NERV he did poorly in school and was apathetic and almost nihilistic of life.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Mon Oct 29, 2007 2:57 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: 7th-chan wrote: Bottom line: Shinji's my kind of fuck-up.

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Mon Oct 29, 2007 4:35 pm Shinji really doesn't hate others. Throughout the entire series, being a rebel to him would be completely out of character. He hates no one but himself. He copes in life just by doing what other people wanted, because he is, in a sense, wimpy. He can't make decisions for himself, because he is afraid of what other people might think of him. So he continues to go along with the norm, hoping to be accepted by people. He seeks self comfort from others, becuase he isn't willing, or more likely, hasn't learnt to accept himself yet for who he is.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 29, 2007 6:41 pm The Kaworu & Shinji quote I referenced earlier, which also relates to something I'm about to bring up: Episode 24' wrote: Shinji: All sorts of things have happened since I came here. Before coming here, I used to live with my teacher. The days were peaceful. Nothing ever happened. I only had to exist there. But that was okay, because there was nothing I had to do. KAWORU: Did you hate people? Shinji: Not really, I think it didn't matter, one way or another. But I did hate my father.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 29, 2007 7:14 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: On the contrary, he cares deeply about other people

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Wed Oct 31, 2007 10:53 am I think its more along the lines of him getting caught up in his own thoughts, and not caring about his current reality. He's the kind of person you need to push to really get him going, being very timid and closed up in his own little world. It seems as though he needs the approval of others to perform any actions at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Oct 31, 2007 3:45 pm sounds a little bit like someone i know.....*cough cough*
